title: Node
description: A visual element representing a system component in an architecture view. Nodes can reference DNA primitives from other layers via the `source` field.

id.description: Unique identifier for this node within the view.

name.description: Display name shown on the diagram.

type.description: The kind of component this node represents. Drives visual styling.

status.description: Lifecycle status of this component. Drives visual styling in the viewer: proposed (dashed/dim), planned (solid/greyed), deployed (full color), running (full color + pulse).

source.description: Reference to a DNA primitive in another layer. Format: `<layer>:<primitive-type>:<name>`. Used for cross-layer traceability and auto-derivation.

position.description: Position on the canvas in pixels.

size.description: Dimensions on the canvas in pixels.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this node represents.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs for adapter-specific or display-specific data.

examples.name: Primary DB
examples.description: Postgres 15 database for lending domain data.
